user_query:
We had a blip in our Dockerized services. Read the inputs under input/ and produce two machine-checkable plans plus a short summary. Inputs to read: - input/containers.json — list of containers with fields: name, state (running|exited|restarting), restartCount (number), composeProject (string or null) - input/compose_projects.json — list of compose projects with their services. Each project has: name, services (array of {name, replicasWanted, replicasRunning, state}) - input/logs_.txt — optional text files with recent logs for some containers (if the file exists, use it; if it doesn’t, treat as no fatal signals). Rules for restart_plan.json: 1) A container goes into to_restart if: - state is "exited" or "restarting"; OR - state is "running" AND its logs file (if present) contains any of these case-insensitive fatal patterns: "fatal", "panic:", or "outofmemoryerror". 2) If restartCount > 5, the container must go to investigate instead of to_restart (even if fatal patterns are present). Do not list the same container in both lists. 3) All other containers go to skipped. 4) Sort the container names in each list alphabetically. 5) Include a "notes" string summarizing counts (free-form text is fine, but the file must include this key). Rules for compose_actions.json: 1) For each compose project, if any service has replicasRunning < replicasWanted, include that project name under compose_up. 2) Otherwise, do not include the project in compose_up. 3) Set compose_down to an empty array []. 4) Sort project names in compose_up alphabetically. Deliverables to write: - output/restart_plan.json with exactly these top-level keys: {"to_restart": [...], "investigate": [...], "skipped": [...], "notes": "..."} - output/compose_actions.json with exactly these top-level keys: {"compose_up": [...], "compose_down": []} - output/summary.md — a brief human-readable summary that includes these exact count lines on separate lines: "to_restart: ", "investigate: ", "skipped: ", and "compose_up:

". Also list the names included under to_restart and compose_up somewhere in the file. Use only the data in the input/ files and the rules above. Write all outputs under output/ using the exact filenames and keys specified. Sort all name lists alphabetically.
